case_title: ANTHONY E. HELD VS. GANZ, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: OTHER NON EXEMPT COMPLAINTS, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF HELD, ANTHONY E. PH.D, P.E. AS TO DEFENDANT GANZ, INC. DOES 1 TO 150, INCL. SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR OCT-23-2009 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JUL-27-2009 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         OCT-08-2009
SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT, PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF HELD, ANTHONY E. PH.D, P.E. SERVED MAY-27-2009, CERTIFIED OR REGISTERED MAIL - OUT OF STATE AS TO DEFENDANT GANZ, INC.

NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, AFFIDAVIT, DECLARATION FILED BY PLAINTIFF HELD, ANTHONY E. PH.D, P.E. HEARING SET FOR AUG-11-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND CONSENT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE REFORMULATION PROVISION INCLUDING THE WARNING REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH PROP 65. THE PENALTY PROVISIONS AND THE ATTORNEYS' FEES ARE REASONABLE. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189
MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-11-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301
THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: - JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF CONSENT JUDGMENT - SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED APPROVING CONSENT JUDGMENT AND JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF CONSENT JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF HELD, ANTHONY E. PH.D, P.E.
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT DOES 1 TO 150, INCL.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-23-2009 IS OFF CALENDAR JUDGMENT ON FILE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65
Off Calendar Aug-11-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF ANTHONY HELD'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and Consent Judgment IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE REFORMULATION PROVISION INCLUDING THE WARNING REQUIRED BY THE SETTLEMENT COMPLIES WITH PROP 65. THE PENALTY PROVISIONS AND THE ATTORNEYS' FEES ARE REASONABLE. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 = (301/PJB)
